title: This Presidential Election (Hillary for Pres) - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about the 2016 US presidential election, predicting Trump will win but saying he will vote for Hillary Clinton anyway. He frames Trump as a useful 'accelerator' for societal change and the dying TV era, critiques Clinton's record while defending her character, and outlines his evolving third-party journey from Libertarian to eventually Green Party.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Trump will win due to the Access Hollywood tape and other scandals paradoxically energizing his base
a useful accelerator for societal change; the perfect embodiment of the old world falling apart
Trump is the last gasp of TV culture; in four years TV stars and TV-driven politics will be gone
not a bad person — just a smart person who played the game; deserves respect as highly qualified
voting for Hillary despite reservations; she's reasonable and may get more bold in a second term
fracturing because wealthy elites are losing their low-information religious base to Trump
will become the new Republicans; no longer supporting them after this cycle
plans to vote Green next election; better represents environment and wealth inequality
electing a woman is historically significant and will expose sexism the way Obama's election exposed racism
proud of voting Gary Johnson before it was popular; it was the right move at the time
